title: Family members list — manual test catalog
no_authentication_scope: No login or authenticated session is required for this screen. The viewer and each family member row are identified via the QA harness only: seeded `family_harness_id` and per-row `member_harness_id` bound to the app install (or equivalent: staging deep link query params / environment flag). Preconditions name harness slots (e.g. member A = `harness_m1`)—never passwords, tokens, or sign-in.
prerequisites: See `no_authentication_scope` for how viewer and members are identified without login.

Staging build with Firebase Remote Config readable/overridable: family_list_refresh_interval_s (default 5), low_battery_threshold_pct (default 10), place_stay_minutes (default 20), walk_vehicle_hysteresis_s (default 6).
Safety badge artwork and state names must match the product design table (not in Jira); until attached, assert placeholder state keys from engineering.
Health metrics for members can be injected via Apple Health / Health Connect test doubles or backend fixture timestamps as agreed with the team.

deprecated_test_cases.reason: Prior draft assumed signed-in user; feature scope is no-auth. Replaced by KUDO-35-TC-001 through KUDO-35-TC-006.
Prior draft mixed auth with refresh checks. Replaced by KUDO-35-TC-010 through KUDO-35-TC-013.

test_cases.title: Not safe block above safe; A–Z within each block
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. At least 3 members: two Not safe (names out of order alphabetically) and two Safe (out of order).
steps: Open Family members list.
Record order of all rows except You.
Within Not safe, confirm names sort A→Z.
Within Safe, confirm names sort A→Z.
Confirm every Not safe row appears above every Safe row.
test_cases.expected_result: Ordering: all Not safe first (A→Z among them), then all Safe (A→Z among them). You excluded from this check (see TC-002).

test_cases.title: “You” row always last regardless of safety
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Include scenarios: (a) You is Not safe, (b) You is Safe, (c) You is only member.
steps: For each harness scenario, open the list.
Locate the row labeled as the current viewer (You).
Confirm it is the bottom row in every scenario.
test_cases.expected_result: You is always the last row whether Not safe or Safe; still last when alone.

test_cases.title: Tie / duplicate display names — stable secondary sort
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Two different members share the same display name prefix (or full duplicate if product allows).
steps: Open list.
Observe order among duplicates.
Confirm list remains stable across refresh (no row swapping without data change).
test_cases.expected_result: Order is deterministic per product rules (e.g. stable harness id); Not safe / Safe / You rules still hold.

test_cases.title: Unicode / diacritics in alphabetical sort
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member names include unicode (e.g. Å, é, 中文) per locale collation product uses.
steps: Open list.
Compare order to expected collation for that locale.
test_cases.expected_result: Sorting matches product locale rules within Not safe and Safe blocks.

test_cases.title: Single non-you member + You
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Exactly one other member besides You.
steps: Open list.
Confirm other member is first, You last.
test_cases.expected_result: Two rows: other member on top, You at bottom.

test_cases.title: All members Safe — You still last
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. All harness members Safe.
steps: Open list.
Confirm full list is alphabetical Safe block with You last.
test_cases.expected_result: Only Safe section (A→Z) then You.

test_cases.title: Reload when returning from background
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Ability to change member order or safety from fixture while app is backgrounded.
steps: On list screen, note order.
Background app; change data so order should change.
Return to foreground on same screen.
Confirm list reflects new order without force-quit.
test_cases.expected_result: Resume from background triggers reload; ordering and badges update.

test_cases.title: Foreground refresh interval — default 5 s from RC
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. RC `family_list_refresh_interval_s` at default (5). Data can change without navigation.
steps: Stay on list in foreground.
Change fixture affecting a visible field (e.g. activity line).
Wait for at least one interval without leaving screen.
Observe update.
test_cases.expected_result: UI picks up changes on the RC interval (~5 s default) while foregrounded.

test_cases.title: Foreground interval respects non-default RC
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Staging override: set refresh interval to a different value (e.g. 15 s).
steps: With stopwatch, confirm updates align with new interval.
Restore default and spot-check.
test_cases.expected_result: Refresh cadence follows RC; restoring default restores ~5 s behavior.

test_cases.title: No duplicate rows or broken scroll after refresh
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Mid-scroll on a long list.
steps: Scroll to middle.
Wait through at least one refresh cycle.
Scroll top to bottom.
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate member rows; scroll remains usable; no permanent loading stuck state.

test_cases.title: Avatar present on every row
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth.
steps: Open list.
For each member row verify avatar image/placeholder renders.
test_cases.expected_result: Each row shows an avatar per design.

test_cases.title: Name single line; trimmed/ellipsized when long
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. At least one very long display name.
steps: Open list.
Confirm name stays on one line.
Confirm overflow is trimmed or ellipsized (no wrap to second line in name field).
test_cases.expected_result: Name max one line; excess trimmed per platform pattern.

test_cases.title: Safety badge per member per product table
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Members in distinct safety states covered by design table.
steps: For each state, open row.
Compare badge to design matrix.
test_cases.expected_result: Badge matches correct row for each safety state (visual + semantics per table).

test_cases.title: Speed hidden when not in vehicle
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member in walking or stationary (not vehicle) with GPS.
steps: Open list.
Inspect row for speed/speedometer element.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed not shown.

test_cases.title: Speed shown only in vehicle
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member in sustained In a vehicle state (>6 km/h, hysteresis satisfied—see activity cases).
steps: Open list.
Confirm speed is visible for that member.
Confirm unit/format matches spec.
test_cases.expected_result: Speed appears only for vehicle state.

test_cases.title: “Low battery” exact copy below RC threshold
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Control member device battery report: below default 10% with RC `low_battery_threshold_pct` = 10.
steps: Set battery < 10%.
Open list.
Read indicator text.
test_cases.expected_result: Exact visible copy includes “Low battery” as specified.

test_cases.title: No low battery at or above threshold boundary
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Battery at 10% or above with default RC threshold 10%.
steps: Open list.
Confirm no low-battery row indicator (per product: strictly below threshold).
test_cases.expected_result: At threshold or above: no “Low battery” indicator.

test_cases.title: RC threshold change updates visibility
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Staging can set `low_battery_threshold_pct` to e.g. 20%.
steps: With battery at 15%, default threshold: note visibility.
Raise threshold to 20%: indicator should appear.
Lower threshold: indicator clears.
test_cases.expected_result: Threshold follows RC.

test_cases.title: Health hidden when member In a vehicle
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member has today’s health samples but is In a vehicle.
steps: Open list.
Confirm no health indicators (steps/sleep/HR/BP/BS) on row.
test_cases.expected_result: No health chips/lines while in vehicle.

test_cases.title: “Today” uses viewer local timezone
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Viewer TZ differs from UTC; member has samples near UTC midnight boundary.
steps: Set viewer TZ and fixture timestamps.
Open list.
Confirm only today-in-viewer-TZ metrics contribute.
test_cases.expected_result: Health “today” aligns with viewer timezone day window.

test_cases.title: At most two indicator types; latest values per type
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member not in vehicle; fixture has fresh values for 3+ indicator types today.
steps: Open list.
Count distinct health indicator types shown (max 2).
Confirm values are latest per type.
test_cases.expected_result: Shows up to two indicator types with latest values for each.

test_cases.title: Ambiguity pack — product chose 2 types / 2 latest readings (sign-off)
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Spec text mixed “2 indicators” vs “latest 3 values from 2 indicators”; harness documents product decision.
steps: Confirm UI shows at most two metric types.
If product shows two readings from one type + one from another, document as pass per signed-off rule.
test_cases.expected_result: Behavior matches signed-off interpretation recorded in test evidence.

test_cases.title: Tie-break same timestamp: BP before BS before Sleep before HR before Steps
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Fixture: two types share identical owner timestamp; also full chain tie scenario if possible.
steps: Open list.
Verify which types appear follow priority BP → Blood sugar → Sleep → Heart rate → Steps.
test_cases.expected_result: Priority order respected when timestamps tie.

test_cases.title: Formatting: Steps, Sleep, HR, BP, Blood sugar
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Known sample values: steps 1843; sleep 5h34m; HR 87 BPM; BP 123/85 mmHg; blood sugar 6.6 mmol/L (note BP spelling mmHg).
steps: Open list for member with each type.
Compare strings to spec formats.
test_cases.expected_result: Formats match: e.g. “1,843”, “5hr34m” or product-approved sleep format, “87 BPM”, “123/85 mmHG” per ticket copy, “6.6 mmol/L”.

test_cases.title: No fabricated values when no data
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member not in vehicle; no health data today.
steps: Open list.
Confirm no placeholder random numbers.
test_cases.expected_result: Empty or hidden health region—no fake metrics.

test_cases.title: Activity uses at most two lines; overflow trimmed
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member online; compose long activity string via multiple simultaneous signals if harness allows.
steps: Open list.
Measure activity text lines (max 2).
Confirm ellipsis/clamp if needed.
test_cases.expected_result: ≤2 lines; trimming per design.

test_cases.title: Stayed place — duration ≥ RC minutes (default 20)
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. GPS at “other” place with dwell ≥ 20 min (default RC `place_stay_minutes`).
steps: Open list.
Confirm copy reflects stayed-before branch.
Shorten dwell below threshold: copy switches to never-stayed branch.
test_cases.expected_result: Stay classification follows RC minutes.

test_cases.title: Walking vs vehicle hysteresis (RC default 6 s)
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Speed oscillates around 6 km/h; RC `walk_vehicle_hysteresis_s` = 6.
steps: Flip speed briefly across boundary; confirm no immediate flip-flop.
Hold new state >6 s; confirm state changes.
test_cases.expected_result: State changes only after sustained new movement class per hysteresis.

test_cases.title: Precise location off reflected when applicable
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Toggle precise location off for member device.
steps: Open list.
Confirm activity mentions reduced precision per product copy.
test_cases.expected_result: Precise-off state appears in composed status when location exists.

test_cases.title: No GPS / call / phone use / weather / late night fragments when online rules apply
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Exercise: no GPS; in-call; screen active; heavy weather; local time 22:00 (late night window).
steps: For each harness state, open list.
Confirm expected fragments appear in activity (per product composition rules).
test_cases.expected_result: Online activity composition includes relevant elements; still not offline template.

test_cases.title: Low battery in activity matches row low-battery definition
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member online, battery below threshold.
steps: Open list.
Confirm activity composition treats low battery consistently with row indicator rules.
test_cases.expected_result: Activity low-battery yes/no aligns with < threshold definition.

test_cases.title: Offline: last seen < 1 min → “just now”
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member offline; last seen <60s ago.
steps: Open list.
Read activity.
test_cases.expected_result: Activity is only offline pattern; includes “No connection, last seen just now” (or equivalent per exact copy table).

test_cases.title: Offline: 1–59 min → “Xm ago”
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Last seen 5 minutes ago.
steps: Open list.
test_cases.expected_result: “No connection, last seen 5m ago” (minute bucket).

test_cases.title: Offline: hours branch (<24h) → “Yh ago”
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Last seen 3 hours ago (same calendar day policy per product).
steps: Open list.
test_cases.expected_result: Hour bucket text matches spec (y = hours mod 24 per ticket).

test_cases.title: Offline: days branch (<30 days) → “Zd ago”
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Last seen 5 days ago.
steps: Open list.
test_cases.expected_result: Day bucket text matches spec (z = days mod 30 per ticket).

test_cases.title: Offline: very old → long time branch
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Last seen >30 days or product-defined long-time cutoff.
steps: Open list.
test_cases.expected_result: “No connection long time ago” (or product exact string).

test_cases.title: When online, offline template not used
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member online with rich activity inputs.
steps: Open list.
Confirm activity is not the offline last-seen sentence.
test_cases.expected_result: No “No connection, last seen …” while online.

test_cases.title: Offline suppresses non-offline activity fragments
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member offline; would otherwise show place/movement if online.
steps: Open list.
Confirm only offline last-seen string; no speed/health (per spec: health hidden in vehicle only applies when not offline row rules—confirm product: offline row shows offline string only).
test_cases.expected_result: Offline row shows offline template; no mixed online place/speed/health clutter per product sign-off.

test_cases.title: All four RC keys observable in one staging session
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. RC overrides for refresh, battery %, stay minutes, hysteresis.
steps: Change each key; observe corresponding UI/behavior.
Revert to defaults.
test_cases.expected_result: Each parameter maps to described behavior; defaults 5s / 10% / 20m / 6s.

test_cases.title: Walking speed range does not show vehicle speed UI
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Speed 1–6 km/h sustained.
steps: Open list.
Confirm walking branch, no vehicle speed display.
test_cases.expected_result: No vehicle-only speed in walking band.

test_cases.title: Brief vehicle spike under hysteresis does not flip row
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. <6 s vehicle burst then back to walk.
steps: Observe list row movement/vehicle indicators.
Confirm hysteresis prevents flicker.
test_cases.expected_result: No spurious vehicle state.

test_cases.title: Place — at home / work / saved places (geofence)
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: viewer = `harness_viewer`, family = `harness_family_alpha`. Members identified only by harness row IDs—no auth. Member GPS/fixture places `harness_m1` inside home, then work, then a saved-place geofence per product definitions.
steps: For each place type, open Family members list.
Read activity line for that member.
Confirm copy matches product strings for home / work / saved-place branches.
test_cases.expected_result: Place-based clause reflects home, work, or saved place when geofence matches; still obeys two-line max and online (non-offline) composition rules.

test_cases.title: Place — other location never stayed before
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: member at an “other” coordinate with no prior stay history in fixture.
steps: Open list.
Confirm activity uses the “never stayed before” branch for other places.
test_cases.expected_result: Never-stayed copy appears (per product); not classified as stayed until dwell ≥ RC stay minutes.

test_cases.title: Phone — in call appears in composed activity when online
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: member device in active call state; member online with location as needed for composition.
steps: Start/stimulate call state for `harness_m1`.
Open list.
Confirm in-call fragment appears in activity per product copy.
test_cases.expected_result: In-call state contributes to activity string; not replaced by offline template while online.

test_cases.title: Phone — using phone (unlocked / touch) in activity
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: screen unlocked and/or touch events firing for member device; not in offline mode.
steps: Open list while harness reports active phone use.
Confirm corresponding fragment in activity line.
test_cases.expected_result: Active phone-use signal appears in composed status per rules.

test_cases.title: Phone stationary (motion) reflected when online
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: motion sensor reports stationary vs moving for member device.
steps: Set stationary true; open list — note activity.
Set stationary false; refresh — note change if product includes this facet.
test_cases.expected_result: Stationary/motion facet matches harness and product copy when included in the formula.

test_cases.title: Movement — no GPS vs no movement with location
test_cases.preconditions: Harness: (a) no GPS fix for member, (b) GPS fix with speed ~0 (no move).
steps: For (a), open list — confirm “no location” / no GPS branch.
For (b), confirm no-move branch distinct from walking/vehicle.
test_cases.expected_result: Correct movement sub-branch for missing GPS vs zero movement with fix.
